question: A 60-year-old man with hypertension and diabetes presents with crushing chest pain radiating to the left arm for 2 hours. ECG shows ST elevation in V1-V4. What is the most likely diagnosis?

media.description: ECG shows ST elevation 3mm in V1-V4

explanations.correct: ST elevation in V1-V4 indicates anteroseptal STEMI. This is a medical emergency requiring immediate reperfusion therapy. Door-to-balloon time should be < 90 minutes.
explanations.A: Unstable Angina presents without ST elevation. ECG may show ST depression or T-wave inversion, but not ST elevation.
explanations.C: Aortic dissection may cause chest pain but ECG typically does not show ST elevation unless coronary ostia are involved. Would expect widened mediastinum on CXR.
explanations.D: Acute pericarditis shows diffuse ST elevation (not localized to V1-V4) and PR depression.
explanations.E: Pulmonary embolism may cause chest pain but ECG shows S1Q3T3 pattern or right heart strain, not anterior ST elevation.

learningPearl: Door-to-balloon time in STEMI should be less than 90 minutes for optimal outcomes.
